message:
fix(widgets): extend NoStyling degradation to rule and spinner widgets

Sixth wave of the NoStyling/Skeleton degradation cleanup across
ftui-widgets.  Two more widgets were baking their configured
Style values into cells without first checking
deg.apply_styling(), so they'd emit colored glyphs under
NoStyling.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/rule.rs

- Widget::render(): snapshot deg up front and precompute two
  local style variables:
    rule_style  = if deg.apply_styling() { self.style }
                  else { Style::default() };
    title_style = if deg.apply_styling() {
                    self.title_style.unwrap_or(self.style)
                  } else { Style::default() };
- Replace every internal use of self.style / self.title_style
  with the local variables:
    - The title-fits-but-no-rule-chars branch uses title_style
      instead of the ad hoc
      `let ts = self.title_style.unwrap_or(self.style)` binding
      it had before.
    - Left and right title-block padding cells use rule_style
      instead of self.style.
    - Title draw inside the title block uses title_style.
- fill_rule_char() inherits the new rule_style indirectly
  because its caller now passes the correct Cell already.
  (The helper itself didn't need changes.)
- No tests added — the existing render_* tests continue to
  pass because they all exercise the default DegradationLevel
  (Full), where apply_styling() is true and the local
  variables are identical to self.style / self.title_style.

crates/ftui-widgets/src/spinner.rs

- Widget::render(): same pattern — deg snapshot +
  render_content() short-circuit, then a local `style` bound
  conditionally on apply_styling().  The existing
  draw_text_span call passes `style` instead of self.style.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
